_meta.title: The unified nav-rail family — ui-nav-rail + ui-nav-rail-group + ui-nav-rail-item (@agent-ui/app), one closed `collapse` enum (menu · drill-in · icon-popover), plus the two consumer migrations (ui-settings, the docs site)
_meta.traces: TKT-0030 (reconcile ruling) · TKT-0029 (site-nav v2, SUBSUMED) · ADR-0130 (proposed) · SPEC nav-rail-family.spec.md (SPEC-R1..R10) · LLD nav-rail-family.lld.md (LLD-C1..C12)
_meta.notes: PLAN mode (docs-only intake; build fans out later). Phase 1 (n1*/n2*) ships the family and gates it FIRST. Phase 2 (n3a/n3b) is the two consumer migrations, parallel-safe once Phase 1 lands (ui-settings and the site nav share no file). Phase 3 (n4a) is the serial barrel/budget integration, after both migrations. n5 is the per-element independent component-reviewer gate. Open Kim-facing forks (family scope, the collapse-name reuse, content model, a11y role derivation, drill-in ownership, icon-popover composition + tag realization) are ALL carried in ADR-0130 as proposed clauses with firm recommendations — this decomposition does not restate them, only gates the build on the ADR's ratification. Constraints named in the dispatch brief: docs + the two tickets only this wave; NEVER touch tokens.css; a concurrent seat (intake-m2-surfaces) may also be minting ADR numbers the same day — this wave took 0130 as the next free number at write time, noted as a race in the report, not resolved here; the concurrent controls/select and M2-docs seats own their own files, untouched by this intake.

nodes.label: One nav-rail concept, three collapse modes — the settings rail and the site nav become two consumers of ONE shared component instead of two independent hand-rolled implementations
nodes.justify: grouping — the milestone root; its subsystems are the family itself, the two consumer migrations, and the final integration slice

nodes.label: ui-nav-rail + ui-nav-rail-group + ui-nav-rail-item — the three elements' behavior
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the family's own TS behavior, ahead of its CSS/descriptors/gates

nodes.label: ui-nav-rail element — app/src/controls/nav-rail/nav-rail.ts: UIElement, reflected closed-enum `collapse` (menu|drill-in|icon-popover, 'menu' index-0), role derivation from item shape (all-href -> navigation, all-bare -> tablist), selection-commit listener (button-shaped items only, emits select/change, never on link navigation or first-run), icon-popover one-open-at-a-time coordination (closes sibling group menus on a child ui-menu 'toggle' to open)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; UINavRailElement extends UIElement (formAssociated:false, light-DOM default, host-as-list, render() stays void); `collapse` reflects and coerces an out-of-set/unset value to 'menu' (values[0] fallback, no throw — SPEC-R1 AC2); a connected() effect (re-armed on child mutation) derives internals.role = 'navigation' when every ui-nav-rail-item descendant has a non-empty href, or 'tablist' when every one is bare (mixed sets default to navigation, undefined per SPEC §7, never throws); a delegated click listener commits selection only for bare (button-shaped) items — sets selected on the activated item, clears siblings, emits select+change {value} on the rail — and does NOT intercept a href-bearing item's native navigation; a listener on child ui-menu 'toggle' events (icon-popover mode) closes every OTHER open child group menu when one opens

nodes.label: ui-nav-rail-group element — app/src/controls/nav-rail/nav-rail-group.ts: UIElement, `label` prop (context-label heading), collapse-mode-aware child composition (plain items wrapper in menu/drill-in; an internally-composed ui-menu wrapping 2+ items in icon-popover mode, a lone item stays a plain icon button)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; UINavRailGroupElement extends UIElement; `label` ('' default, non-reflected) renders a [data-part=context-label] heading when non-empty; reads the ancestor ui-nav-rail's `collapse` reactively (this.closest('ui-nav-rail')?.collapse) to pick composition mode at connect (post-connect collapse changes take effect on next reconnect, documented limitation, the ui-app-shell isolated-is-connect-time-only precedent); icon-popover mode with >=2 ui-nav-rail-item children composes ONE internal ui-menu (the group's label/icon as trigger, children's content re-expressed as [role=menuitem] elements preserving href-vs-bare activation semantics) — a 0- or 1-item group never builds a popover it does not need; never throws on 0 items

nodes.label: ui-nav-rail-item element — app/src/controls/nav-rail/nav-rail-item.ts: UIElement, `href` (reflected string, '' default) + `selected` (reflected boolean), renders <a href> when href is set (aria-current on selected) or <button role=tab via internals> when bare (aria-selected via internals, tracking selected)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; UINavRailItemElement extends UIElement; href non-empty renders an <a href="…"> wrapping the default/label slot content, reactively toggling aria-current="page" with `selected` (SPEC-R3 AC1); href empty renders a <button type="button">, internals.role='tab', internals.ariaSelected tracking `selected` (SPEC-R3 AC2, set via ElementInternals never a host attribute); href toggled post-connect reactively re-renders the <a>/<button> swap (not one-shot); supports [slot=leading data-role=icon] and [slot=trailing data-role=tag] per anatomy.md's slot/role axes (the tag slot REALIZES anatomy.md's reserved `tag` role); the shared non-color-alone active indicator (a real border-inline-start + a `selected` internals custom state) applies identically to both <a> and <button> shapes

nodes.label: the shared anatomy — CSS, descriptors, gates, catalog disposition (all three modes)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: what proves the three elements meet the fleet DoD across all three collapse modes

nodes.label: collapse="menu" narrow disclosure — nav-rail.ts (menu-mode path) + nav-rail.css: the site's existing zero-JS <details>/<summary> mechanism (_page.css/buildNav precedent), promoted into the component, establishing its own @container width query
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` (Chromium AND WebKit) proves: at/above the rail's own container-width threshold the full group/item list renders and the disclosure is inert chrome (summary hidden); below it, the list is not directly visible and a single <summary data-part=trigger> (naming the current item, a CSS chevron) is, with the assertion biting on a non-collapsing fixed-layout negative control (SPEC-R5 AC1); activating the trigger (click or Enter/Space) opens an overlaying (non-reflowing) dropdown panel dismissable via Escape or outside-click (SPEC-R5 AC2)

nodes.label: collapse="icon-popover" mechanics — nav-rail.ts/nav-rail-group.ts/nav-rail.css: icon-only item rendering (label visually hidden, kept as the accessible name) + the ui-menu group flyout + cross-group one-open coordination
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` (Chromium AND WebKit) proves: every item in icon-popover mode shows only its icon with a correct AX-tree accessible name (SPEC-R8 AC1); a group's icon trigger opens its item list as an anchored popover with roving keyboard focus (Arrow keys) that commits-and-closes on item activation, the ui-menu cross-engine suite's own assertions ported (SPEC-R8 AC2); opening group B's popover while group A's is open closes A (never two simultaneously open), with a biting negative control that drops the coordination listener (SPEC-R8 AC3); every real open-state transition (commit/Escape/outside-click/programmatic) announces via the SAME toggle+close pair ui-menu already emits, no new event name (SPEC-R8 AC4)

nodes.label: nav-rail.css — controls/nav-rail/nav-rail.css: shared anatomy (Pattern size-class, control-height rows), the non-color-alone active indicator + forced-colors repaint, the name|tag two-column row + narrow truncate-never-wrap
nodes.accept: `npm run check`/browser green; single-file sectioned (ADR-0003): a :where(ui-nav-rail) token block declaring ONLY --ui-nav-rail-* (ported/renamed from --ui-settings-rail-*) then @scope (ui-nav-rail); [collapse=…] attribute-selector branches for the menu/icon-popover paths live in this ONE file, never a second stylesheet; forced-colors:active repaints the active-item border to a system color, never vanishing (SPEC-R4); a wide tag-bearing item shows name-left/tag-right space-between (SPEC-R6 AC1); narrowed below its content width the tag truncates with an ellipsis and the row height stays single-line/unchanged, never wrapping (SPEC-R6 AC2)

nodes.label: descriptors — controls/nav-rail/{nav-rail.md, nav-rail-group.md, nav-rail-item.md}
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; all three validate against the ADR-0004 frontmatter schema (nav-rail.md: tier:pattern, extends:UIElement, attributes:[collapse], customStates:[] — states live on the item child; nav-rail-group.md: attributes:[label]; nav-rail-item.md: attributes:[href,selected], customStates:[selected], aria.role documented as conditional per SPEC-R3); the contract↔props trip-wire equals finalize(UINavRailElement)/finalize(UINavRailGroupElement)/finalize(UINavRailItemElement) GREEN for all three

nodes.label: gates — controls/nav-rail/{nav-rail.test.ts, nav-rail.browser.test.ts}
nodes.accept: `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green; jsdom proves collapse enum coercion, role derivation (all-href/all-bare cases), selection-commit emits select+change exactly once per genuine activation (never on a deep-link `selected` write or on link-shaped navigation), the tag trailing-slot renders + truncates against the REAL BUILT css (not just the source rule, the ADR-0106 CSS-comment-pitfall lesson); browser (Chromium AND WebKit) proves whole-shape per mode, the n2a/n2b legs, and the n2c forced-colors/name-tag-row legs, each with a biting negative control

nodes.label: catalog EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entries — a2ui/src/catalog/default/index.test.ts
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0; all three tags (ui-nav-rail, ui-nav-rail-group, ui-nav-rail-item) resolve via a cited EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry ("app-tier nav chrome, PRD-D2, never agent-emittable" — the ui-app-shell/ui-master-detail/ui-settings precedent) rather than an unresolved-row failure (SPEC-R1 AC1)

nodes.label: the two consumer migrations (parallel-safe once n1/n2 land)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the reconciliation proven by consumption, per TKT-0030's own acceptance criterion

nodes.label: ui-settings migration — app/src/controls/settings/{settings.ts,settings.css,settings.md}: the hand-rolled [data-part=rail]/[data-part=rail-item] markup replaced by composing <ui-nav-rail collapse="drill-in"> inside the existing ui-master-detail list pane
nodes.accept: `npm run check` + `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green; settings.ts's #build() constructs <ui-nav-rail collapse=drill-in> once (idempotent) + one <ui-nav-rail-item href="" selected="…"> per schema section, appended to it, replacing the manual button/click-listener construction; settings listens for the rail's OWN select/change event instead of per-button clicks; #markActiveRailItem() becomes "set selected on the matching item"; settings.css's [data-part=rail-item] rules are DELETED (0 bespoke rail CSS remaining, only [data-part=panel]/[data-part=notice] survive); ui-master-detail's composition + narrow drill-in/back behavior is UNCHANGED (its existing browser suite re-runs green with no edit, SPEC-R7 AC1); the AX tree now reads role=tablist/role=tab/aria-selected (SPEC-R9 AC2, a deliberate git-diff-visible a11y correction, not a silent behavior change); the existing schema/store reconnect-guard discipline (#builtSchema/#builtStore) is untouched

nodes.label: site-nav migration — site/pages/_page.{ts,css}, site-nav.browser.test.ts: buildNav()/the hand NAV array replaced by composing <ui-nav-rail collapse="menu"> derived from sitemap.json
nodes.accept: `npm run check`(+check:site) + `npm run build` + `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` green; _page.ts's buildNav() constructs <ui-nav-rail collapse=menu> from sitemap.json's L1/L2 entries grouped by their `section` field (the sitemap's own taxonomy, no curated re-grouping at v1) instead of the hand NAV array, each entry a <ui-nav-rail-item href="…"> carrying slot=trailing data-role=tag = the entry's tag field; currentNavLabel()/activeGroup()/isCurrent() are ported into ui-nav-rail's own collapse=menu disclosure-trigger derivation rather than duplicated in _page.ts; _page.css's nav[data-site-nav] rule block (rail anatomy, disclosure chrome, narrow media query) is DELETED, now living in nav-rail.css; site-nav.browser.test.ts's expected rail-entry count derives from sitemap.json (not NAV.length, SPEC-R10 AC1); site-coverage/site-toc drift gates (independently derived from components/src descriptors) are UNCHANGED and continue to catch an un-navved page (SPEC-R10 AC2); the per-component Permutations/States/API sub-links remain on the page-header tab strip, unchanged (SPEC-R10 AC3, confirmed non-goal)

nodes.label: final integration (serial, after both migrations)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the barrel export + measured size re-base, done once against the fully-adopted tree

nodes.label: app barrel + size re-base — app/src/index.ts, package.json exports (./nav-rail + .css subpath), scripts/measure-size.mjs
nodes.accept: `npm run size` exit 0 with a NEW @agent-ui/app line-item for nav-rail, re-based measured-at-build against the tree with BOTH migrations landed (never guessed in advance); app/src/index.ts exports UINavRailElement/UINavRailGroupElement/UINavRailItemElement; package.json gains ./nav-rail, ./nav-rail.css (bare ./{name} shape, naming.md §8's app/router rule); app/src/layering.test.ts still passes with no widening of the allowed-specifier set (nav-rail imports only @agent-ui/components + its ui-master-detail sibling, never @agent-ui/router, SPEC §5's layering gate)

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer gate — Phase 1, one per new element, pre-commit NO-GO
nodes.accept: component-reviewer verdict recorded GO (zero blockers; COMPOSE >=4 AND REALIZE >=4) on ui-nav-rail, ui-nav-rail-group, and ui-nav-rail-item BEFORE Phase 1 commits; any NO-GO is closed + delta-re-reviewed to GO before n3a/n3b begin

nodes.label: regression-focused review — Phase 2, one per migration, pre-commit NO-GO
nodes.accept: a regression-focused review on the ui-settings migration (n3a) and the site-nav migration (n3b) BEFORE their respective commits, each citing the pre-migration browser suite staying green as primary evidence; any NO-GO is closed + delta-re-reviewed to GO

actions.label: expose ONE closed collapse enum (menu|drill-in|icon-popover) governing the rail's own narrow-width disposition
derive the rail's ARIA role from item shape (href present vs. absent), never from collapse
commit a selection from a button-shaped item as an allow-listed select/change event, never intercepting real navigation
coordinate icon-popover group menus so at most one is open at a time
render a context-label heading and, in icon-popover mode, compose an internal ui-menu flyout for 2+ items
render as a real <a> (link-shaped) or a role=tab <button> (selection-shaped), each with its own correct ARIA state
realize anatomy.md's reserved tag role in the trailing slot, truncating (never wrapping) narrow
collapse into a zero-JS disclosure dropdown below the rail's own container-width threshold (menu mode)
render icon-only items with a correct accessible name and open a group's items via an anchored, roving-focus, commit-and-close popover (icon-popover mode)
style the shared anatomy at Pattern size-class geometry with a non-color-alone, forced-colors-safe active indicator
describe all three elements in descriptors and pass the contract↔props trip-wire
prove every collapse mode's whole-shape + behavior cross-engine with biting negative controls
dispose all three tags via a cited EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry, never an unresolved catalog row
migrate ui-settings onto the shared rail (drill-in), deleting its own bespoke rail CSS, with ui-master-detail's mechanism unchanged
migrate the docs site nav onto the shared rail (menu), sitemap-derived, deleting its own bespoke rail CSS
export the family's public surface and re-base the app package's measured size budget
judge each new element COMPOSE/REALIZE >=4 before Phase 1 commits
judge each migration regression-clean before its commit

edges.why: the group reads its ancestor rail's collapse prop to pick its own composition mode
the rail's selection-commit listener and role derivation depend on the item's href/selected contract
a group relocates its item children's content into its internal ui-menu (icon-popover mode) or a plain wrapper (menu/drill-in)
the menu-mode narrow disclosure is the rail's own connect-time composition
the icon-popover group flyout is the group's own connect-time composition; the rail adds only cross-group coordination
the CSS styles the item's rendered shape (link vs. button) and the tag trailing slot
the descriptor mirrors the element's static props; the trip-wire targets it
the gates prove the menu-mode disclosure cross-engine
the gates prove the icon-popover mechanics + coordination cross-engine
the gates prove the shared anatomy/forced-colors/tag-row legs
the catalog gate scans the shipped descriptors for a resolvable row or allowlist entry
the reviewer gate runs against gate-green elements, never ahead of them
the settings migration composes a REVIEWED, frozen ui-nav-rail contract, never an in-flux one
the settings migration gets its own regression-focused review before its commit
the size re-base is measured against the tree with both reviewed migrations landed
